old_value:
{"id":"solar-controller-noc","title":"Test: TopicPrefix tests never validate prefix resolution logic","description":"internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:140, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:81, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:77

All three TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests use Enabled: false to avoid connection attempts, which causes the publisher to return an empty struct with topicPrefix: "". Every test case asserts pub.topicPrefix != "" which always passes. The table-driven cases give false confidence that prefix resolution logic works, but the assertions never exercise it. If the prefix selection logic were deleted, these tests would still pass.

Fix: For each publisher (MQTT, Solace, SNS), either (a) extract the prefix resolution logic into a standalone function that can be unit tested without connecting, or (b) inject a mock client so the publisher can be created in enabled mode without a real connection, then assert the resolved topicPrefix matches expected values for each table case (param wins over config, config wins over default, default is "solar").

Done when: Each of the three TestNewPublisher_TopicPrefix tests has at least one case where the resolved topicPrefix is checked against a non-empty expected value that differs per table entry, and the test would fail if the prefix resolution logic were removed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jellwood@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:44Z","created_by":"lumberbarons","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:44Z"}

{"id":"solar-controller-24v","title":"Test: TestPublishError only checks no-panic","description":"internal/remotewrite/publisher_test.go:390

TestPublishError configures a server returning 500 errors but has no assertions on the publisher's behavior — it only verifies no panic via time.Sleep. It doesn't check that the publisher remains functional after errors or that the server actually received the request.

Fix: Add assertions that verify (a) the mock server received at least one request, and (b) the publisher can still accept new metrics after the error (publish again and verify the server receives a second request). Use the existing mu-guarded receivedRequests pattern from TestPublishWithMockServer.

Done when: TestPublishError has at least one assertion (assert/require) that verifies the server received a request, and the test would fail if the publisher silently dropped messages instead of sending them.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jellwood@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T20:46:02Z","created_by":"lumberbarons","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:46:02Z"}

{"id":"solar-controller-5m7","title":"Test: Configurer voltage validation ignores error message content","description":"internal/controllers/epever/configurer_test.go:132

TestValidateVoltageParameters defines errorMsg strings like "charging voltage chain violated" per test case but never checks strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg). The assertion only checks err.Error() == "" (line 135), which passes for any non-empty error. If the function returned the wrong validation error, the test would still pass.

Fix: Replace the weak err.Error() == "" check with strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg) to verify each test case triggers the specific expected validation failure.

Done when: The error-path branch in TestValidateVoltageParameters uses strings.Contains(err.Error(), tt.errorMsg) and would fail if validateVoltageParameters returned a different error string than expected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jellwood@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:47Z","created_by":"lumberbarons","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:47Z"}

{"id":"solar-controller-b0q","title":"Test: ServicePropertyMap test has no meaningful output validation","description":"internal/solace/publisher_test.go:148

TestServicePropertyMap calls ServicePropertyMap() with various inputs but only asserts propMap != nil. It never verifies the map contains the expected host, VPN name, username, or password values. A malformed property map would go undetected.

Fix: Assert that the returned property map contains the correct values for each input field. Check that host, VPN name, username, and password appear in the map with the values passed to ServicePropertyMap.

Done when: TestServicePropertyMap asserts at least the host and vpnName values in the returned map match the input arguments, and the test would fail if ServicePropertyMap ignored its parameters.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jellwood@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:59Z","created_by":"lumberbarons","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:59Z"}

{"id":"solar-controller-z6p","title":"Test: Seven tests discard *testing.T with zero assertions","description":"internal/mqtt/publisher_test.go:196, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:126, internal/solace/publisher_test.go:137, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:122, internal/sns/publisher_test.go:132, internal/publishers/factory_test.go:143, internal/publishers/multi_publisher_test.go:147

Seven test functions use _ *testing.T and contain no assertions. They only verify "no panic" which is an extremely weak contract. For example, TestPublish_DisabledPublisher across MQTT/Solace/SNS would pass even if the no-op path silently corrupted state.

Fix: Accept *testing.T in each function and add at least one meaningful assertion. For disabled-publisher Publish tests, assert no side effects occurred (e.g., no messages recorded, no files written). For NoOpPublisher and empty MultiPublisher, assert Publish and Close return without modifying any observable state.

Done when: All seven test functions accept *testing.T (not _ *testing.T) and each contains at least one assertion via t.Error/t.Fatal/assert/require.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jellwood@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:57Z","created_by":"lumberbarons","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:57Z"}

{"id":"solar-controller-4rx","title":"Test: Add config validation tests for file, remotewrite, and SNS publishers","description":"internal/config/config_test.go:8

TestLoad and TestConfig_Validate thoroughly test MQTT, Solace, and Epever configuration but have no test cases for file publisher (enabled with empty filename), remotewrite (missing URL, mutually exclusive auth), or SNS (missing region/topicArn) validation.

Fix: Add test cases to TestLoad for: (1) file publisher enabled with empty filename, (2) remotewrite enabled with missing URL, (3) remotewrite with both basicAuth and bearerToken set, (4) SNS enabled with missing region, (5) SNS enabled with missing topicArn. Each should assert the expected validation error.

Done when: TestLoad has at least one error-path test case for each of the three publishers (file, remotewrite, SNS), and each asserts a specific error message substring.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jellwood@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T20:46:03Z","created_by":"lumberbarons","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:46:03Z"}

{"id":"solar-controller-6ml","title":"Test: Remove redundant sleep before SQS long-poll in integration tests","description":"internal/sns/integration_test.go:104, 164, 221

TestSNSPublisherIntegration uses time.Sleep(2 * time.Second) before each SQS ReceiveMessage call. SQS ReceiveMessage already uses WaitTimeSeconds: 5 for long polling, making the preceding sleep redundant. This adds 6 seconds of unnecessary wait time to the test suite.

Fix: Remove the three time.Sleep(2 * time.Second) calls before ReceiveMessage. The WaitTimeSeconds parameter on ReceiveMessage already handles waiting for message delivery.

Done when: TestSNSPublisherIntegration contains no time.Sleep calls before ReceiveMessage, and the test still passes with WaitTimeSeconds providing the necessary wait.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jellwood@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T20:46:07Z","created_by":"lumberbarons","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:46:07Z"}

{"id":"solar-controller-978","title":"Test: Integration subtests share mutable state via single SQS queue","description":"internal/sns/integration_test.go:23

All three subtests share one SQS queue and publisher. If PublishSingleMessage leaks a message, PublishMultipleMessages receives 4 instead of 3. Subtests cannot run in isolation or in parallel.

Fix: Create a fresh SQS queue (and optionally a fresh SNS topic + subscription) per subtest, scoped with t.Cleanup.

Done when: Each subtest can be run independently (e.g. go test -run TestSNSPublisherIntegration/PublishMultipleMessages) and receive exactly the messages it published, with no dependency on execution order.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jellwood@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T20:51:19Z","created_by":"lumberbarons","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:51:19Z"}
